original_abstract: Conventional extraction techniques are usually based on highly pollutant and/or flammable organic solvents. Therefore, alternative environmentally friendly extraction methods are of particular interest for the recovery of bioactive compounds for their application as food ingredients and/or nutraceuticals. Natural deep eutectic solvents (NADES) are a green and nontoxic attractive alternative to hydroalcoholic extraction. NADES media primarily depends on the intermolecular interactions (hydrogen bonding) among their components to form a eutectic mixture with a much lower final melting point than its individual components. Examples of natural deep eutectic NADES solvents include aqueous solutions (25%–50% water) of choline chloride, sugars, and polyols. This study aimed to investigate the application of two NADES, namely, betaine:triethylene glycol (Bet : TEG) and choline chloride:1,2-propanediol (Chol : Prop), as sustainable green solvents for the extraction of polyphenols from spent coffee ground (SCG), a by-product of coffee processing. In particular, the extraction yield and selectivity were evaluated and compared with conventional green extractions (hot water and a hydroalcoholic solution). In addition, the effect of NADES on the antioxidant and antimicrobial activity of the extracts was investigated. The main outcomes were as follows: (i) NADES were as effective as other conventional green solvents in the extraction of polyphenols with the added advantage of operating at milder temperature conditions, without flammable solvents and with sustainable and natural compounds; (ii) the antimicrobial activity of the NADES extracts was 10 times higher than that of the ethanolic and aqueous extracts. Given the low toxicity of NADES, they could be used as formulation aid for food ingredients.
humanized_abstract: Traditional extraction methods depend on using high amounts of harmful, flammable or toxic organic solvents. Hence, the use of alternative methods which are more environmentally friendly and therefore very relevant for using bioactive compounds derived from plants as food additives or nutraceuticals. Natural Deep Eutectic Solvents (NADES) are eco-friendly and non-toxic alternatives to the hydroalcoholic extraction method. The composition of the media in the NADES depends primarily on the interactions between the molecules of the components (hydrogen bonds) and which form a eutectic mixture with a melting point lower than the individual components. The examples of natural DES media include aqueous mixtures (25%–50% water) of choline chloride, sugars and polyols. Here, two systems were studied namely, betaine:triethylene glycol (Bet : TEG) and choline chloride:1,2-propanediol (Chol : Prop) for using them as alternatives of green sustainable solvents for the extraction of polyphenols from Spent Coffee Ground (SCG) a coffee by-product. The aim of the study was to assess the effect of NADES in terms of the extraction yield and selectivity compared with other conventional green methods such as hot water and hydroalcoholic solutions and to investigate the effect of the NADES media on the antioxidant and antimicrobial activities of the extracted polyphenols. The results revealed that the NADES media exhibited efficacy comparable to those of the conventional green extraction methods while offering more sustainable conditions such as mild temperature and use of non-toxic and sustainable natural compounds. Moreover, the extracted polyphenols from the NADES media showed enhanced antimicrobial activity by a factor of 10 times when compared with that of the ethanolic and aqueous extracts. Hence, the low toxicity of the NADES makes them very favorable as formulation aids for food ingredients.
